arguments.task: Open the official Notion developer documentation and read the strongest first-party pages needed to determine: one-line product description; all documented API authentication methods; whether API access is self-serve free, trial, paid, admin approval, or partner/contact-sales gated; API surface type and breadth; whether an official MCP exists; buildability today; and rate-limit notes. Prefer canonical first-party pages such as developer docs, auth docs, getting started, pricing, rate limits, changelog, and any official MCP or agent docs. Return the final answer as structured notes with supporting live canonical URLs actually visited and read. Do not rely on search-result snippets. If a page is JS-rendered, wait for it to load and then extract the content.

raw_result.description: Notion is an AI-powered workspace that combines notes, docs, wikis, and projects in one platform.
raw_result.auth_notes: Notion documents OAuth 2.0 for public integrations and bearer-style integration tokens for internal integrations; the authorization guide also documents a long-lived personal access token flow for public integrations. No first-party evidence found for API keys or HTTP Basic.
raw_result.gating_reason: Notion publishes self-serve developer docs and public API onboarding, and its pricing page offers a Free plan, so a first toolkit can be built without sales or admin approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with endpoints for blocks, pages, databases/data sources, users, comments, search, and workspace-scoped content operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Notion documents an official hosted Notion MCP server for MCP-compatible clients and agents.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Notion documents an average rate limit of 3 requests per second per integration, with 429 responses and a Retry-After header when exceeded.
raw_result.notable_signals: Well-maintained official developer docs, broad endpoint coverage, official SDKs/docs ecosystem, and an official MCP offering all indicate strong agent/toolkit readiness.
